functionDeclarations.name: list_saved_views
functionDeclarations.description: List existing saved views for a data-table model (use to avoid duplicate names). (GET /api/data-tables/saved-views)

functionDeclarations.name: create_saved_view
functionDeclarations.description: Create a saved view; set is_user_favorite to pin it to the favorites bar. (POST /api/data-tables/saved-views)

model.description: The data-table model this view belongs to, as the exact class string the page uses — e.g. "App\\Models\\Product" for the Products table. Must match the target page.

name.description: Display name shown on the views list / favorites bar.

query_data.description: JSON string of the view state. Shape: { "columns": { "visible": ["sku","name",...] }, "search": "text", "filters": { "type": "standard" }, "filterGroups": "<base64 advanced-filter tree>", "sortBy": "-created_at", "pagination": { "per_page": 50 } }. All keys optional; include only what the view needs. Column keys and filter keys must be valid for that table.

is_user_favorite.description: Pin the view to the table's favorites bar for the current user. Default false, but for a view you create for someone to look at, set this true — an unfavorited view is easy to miss.

is_user_default.description: Make this the user's default view — the table opens on it. Default false.

is_shared.description: Whether other users can see the view. Default true.

color.description: Optional colour label for the favorites chip.

functionDeclarations.name: favorite_saved_view
functionDeclarations.description: Favorite or unfavorite an existing saved view for the current user. (POST /api/data-tables/saved-views/{saved_view}/favorite)

is_favorite.description: true to favorite, false to unfavorite. Omit to toggle.

functionDeclarations.name: set_default_saved_view
functionDeclarations.description: Make a saved view the current user's default (landing) view for its table. (POST /api/data-tables/saved-views/{saved_view}/set-default)
